task.title: Performance of canvas snapshotting on Safari is poor

data.title: Performance of canvas snapshotting on Safari is poor
data.body:
**Describe the bug**

Snapshotting a canvas element in Safari 16.x takes a long time (>20ms). This leads to a frame drop every time the canvas is snapshotted.

**To Reproduce**

Go to the Codsandbox full-screen here https://jqgtld.csb.app/ 

Look at the "Snapshot" stat in the top right corner (or in the console). On my computer it is ~20ms on Safari 16.5. On Chrome, it is between 0 and 0.3ms.

The window size is 1057 x 734 (retina display, so double the dimensions for the canvas)

Here's the codesandbox with the editor: https://codesandbox.io/s/dazzling-roentgen-jqgtld?file=/src/App.js

**Expected behavior**

Overhead for snapshotting to be near 0

**Screenshots**

## Safari

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 23 03 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/1bc6d193-ea1f-4fda-ae03-7ea9c9cf9143)

## Chrome

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 23 55 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/7a3767f7-94a0-467b-9c23-bac305507d66)

## iOS

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 27 57 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/664fc6c4-27b8-43ce-be71-b35b7553716f)


**Additional context**

Environment:

```
System:
    OS: macOS 13.4
    CPU: (8) arm64 Apple M1
    Memory: 51.78 MB / 8.00 GB
Browsers:
    Chrome: 117.0.5938.92
    Safari: 16.5
```

Provided this also affects Safari 17 (I don't know if it does), this affects _every_ Safari-based browser (including everything on iOS). This performance drop means we had to disable canvas recording for Safari on our app which really limits its usefulness.

The larger the canvas, the longer the snapshot time, from what I can tell. Changing `canvasMaxSnapshotDimension` does not appear to make a difference. Safari doesn't even respect the options parameter for `createBitmapImage`: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/createImageBitmap

I'm reasonably confident this comes from the call to `createBitmapImage` here:

https://github.com/highlight/highlight/blob/ed2ea183d0d11781736e2001b01e24e55c33e8cb/frontend/src/__generated/rr/rr.js#L4266-L4269

It's supposed to be asynchronous but it looks like it is blocking in Safari. I wonder if there are other ways to get the image data out of a canvas? I understand there are efforts to use WebRTC for canvas recording? I imagine this could help.

task.body:
**Describe the bug**

Snapshotting a canvas element in Safari 16.x takes a long time (>20ms). This leads to a frame drop every time the canvas is snapshotted.

**To Reproduce**

Go to the Codsandbox full-screen here https://jqgtld.csb.app/ 

Look at the "Snapshot" stat in the top right corner (or in the console). On my computer it is ~20ms on Safari 16.5. On Chrome, it is between 0 and 0.3ms.

The window size is 1057 x 734 (retina display, so double the dimensions for the canvas)

Here's the codesandbox with the editor: https://codesandbox.io/s/dazzling-roentgen-jqgtld?file=/src/App.js

**Expected behavior**

Overhead for snapshotting to be near 0

**Screenshots**

## Safari

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 23 03 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/1bc6d193-ea1f-4fda-ae03-7ea9c9cf9143)

## Chrome

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 23 55 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/7a3767f7-94a0-467b-9c23-bac305507d66)

## iOS

![CleanShot 2023-10-02 at 15 27 57 png](https://github.com/highlight/highlight/assets/3238878/664fc6c4-27b8-43ce-be71-b35b7553716f)


**Additional context**

Environment:

```
System:
    OS: macOS 13.4
    CPU: (8) arm64 Apple M1
    Memory: 51.78 MB / 8.00 GB
Browsers:
    Chrome: 117.0.5938.92
    Safari: 16.5
```

Provided this also affects Safari 17 (I don't know if it does), this affects _every_ Safari-based browser (including everything on iOS). This performance drop means we had to disable canvas recording for Safari on our app which really limits its usefulness.

The larger the canvas, the longer the snapshot time, from what I can tell. Changing `canvasMaxSnapshotDimension` does not appear to make a difference. Safari doesn't even respect the options parameter for `createBitmapImage`: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/createImageBitmap

I'm reasonably confident this comes from the call to `createBitmapImage` here:

https://github.com/highlight/highlight/blob/ed2ea183d0d11781736e2001b01e24e55c33e8cb/frontend/src/__generated/rr/rr.js#L4266-L4269

It's supposed to be asynchronous but it looks like it is blocking in Safari. I wonder if there are other ways to get the image data out of a canvas? I understand there are efforts to use WebRTC for canvas recording? I imagine this could help.

task.title: Autocomplete steals window focus, prevents further typing

data.title: Autocomplete steals window focus, prevents further typing
data.body:
#### Brief summary of issue / Description of requested feature:

Occasionally encountering an issue where autocomplete pops up and prevents typing. Need to esc to remove the autocomplete, but will pop up again with another relevant letter.

#### Steps to reproduce the issue / Reasons for adding feature:

1.  Unknown to why it begins.
2.  Typing a letter that has a corresponding lua command in the autocomplete. 

#### Error output / Expected result of feature

Expected result: Being able to continue typing outside of autocomplete.

#### Extra information, such as Mudlet version, operating system and ideas for how to solve / implement:

Mudlet version: 4.11.2
Windows 10 Home
Video recording: https://youtu.be/qJF0h2MDWzg

task.title: [Bug]: Unable to create redis instance after severe crash.

data.title: [Bug]: Unable to create redis instance after severe crash.
data.body: ### Error Message and Logs A few weeks ago, my vps memory and CPU usage maxed out. After that, Coolify shut down all instances. I was able to bring them back up one by one, but Redis started behaving strangely, I couldn't open its settings page, and now I can't create any new Redis instances (KeyDB works fine). I went into the Redis server’s danger zone and managed to delete it, but I still can't create new Redis instances. Whenever I click “+ New” and select Databases > Redis, I'm redirected to a window with this error, without even seeing the form to create a Redis instance. Wait, this is not cool... There has been an error with the following error message: SQLSTATE[23502]: Not null violation: 7 ERROR: null value in column "redis_password" of relation "standalone_redis" violates not-null constraint DETAIL: Failing row contains (17, rc76pnsl3ozzyfa3z6h8fi51, redis-database-rc76pnsl3ozzyfa3z6h8fi51, null, null, null, exited, redis:7.2, f, null, null, 0, 0, 60, 0, 0, null, 1024, null, App\Models\StandaloneDocker, 0, 4, 2026-04-02 22:14:47, 2026-04-02 22:14:47, f, f, null, null, null, 2025-11-11 17:56:33, f, 0, null, null, 3600). (Connection: pgsql, Host: coolify-db, Port: 5432, Database: coolify, SQL: insert into "standalone_redis" ("uuid", "name", "environment_id", "destination_id", "destination_type", "updated_at", "created_at") values (rc76pnsl3ozzyfa3z6h8fi51, redis-database-rc76pnsl3ozzyfa3z6h8fi51, 4, 0, App\Models\StandaloneDocker, 2026-04-02 22:14:47, 2026-04-02 22:14:47) returning "id") ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Maximize cpu and ram usage for a few minutes. 2. Wait for coolify to crash and shut down everything. 3. My current situation. ### Example Repository URL _No response_ ### Coolify Version v4.0.0-beta.470 ### Are you using Coolify Cloud? No (self-hosted) ### Operating System and Version (self-hosted) Debian 12 ### Additional Information _No response_

task.title: [Enhancement]: WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed service template

data.title: [Enhancement]: WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed service template
data.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Following up on the discussion in #8255 (which was closed to split the scope into two dedicated issues).
As a web agency (Mediastar Web), we manage multiple WordPress sites for clients and we're evaluating Coolify as our central hosting platform. One area where we're missing structured support is a production-ready reference setup for running WordPress on OpenLiteSpeed.
What we'd like to see:
- A reusable service template or stack for WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed, following the existing Coolify template patterns
- Persistent volumes for WordPress files and database data
- Compatibility with Coolify's built-in proxy

task.title: [Enhancement]: Add a reusable email server stack/template for managing client email domains

data.title: [Enhancement]: Add a reusable email server stack/template for managing client email domains
data.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Following up on the discussion in #8255 (which was closed to split the scope into two dedicated issues).
As a web agency (Mediastar Web), we manage email services for multiple client domains and we're evaluating Coolify as our central hosting platform. One area where we're missing structured support is a straightforward way to host and manage client email domains through a reusable template or stack.
What we'd like to see:
- A reusable service template/stack for a self-hosted email server (e.g., based on Docker Mailserver, Mailu, or a similar well-maintained project)
- Persistent volumes for mailboxes, configuration, and certificates
- Compatibility with Coolify's proxy and certificate management

task.title: [Enhancement]: Add Open Replay

data.title: [Enhancement]: Add Open Replay
data.body:
### Request Type

New Service

### Description

[Open Replay](https://openreplay.com/) requires [running a installing script](https://docs.openreplay.com/en/deployment/deploy-docker/#deploy-openreplay) that will "create" the final docker compose file. It would be cool to have open replay as a "one click" (coolify service template) service in coolify.

task.title: [Enhancement]: OAUTH only self registering.

data.title: [Enhancement]: OAUTH only self registering.
data.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Hi, there!

Today users are only able to self-register with OAUTH2 when general self-register is enabled.

The improvement would be:
- Allow users logging in with OAUTH2 accounts to self-register even when general self-registering is disabled;
- Allow admins to restrict users to OAUTH2 only, blocking users from creating and using passwords if they are coming from an OAUTH2 provider (this would allow a single action to suspend users in multiple Coolify instances);

This would allow us to, for example, control who can and cannot login into a Coolify instance using a tool like Authentik (ex: only users within a certain organization).

The ideal world would be SCIM 2.0 or something like LDAP, but this is a good start to block access whenever someone leaves the team.

Thanks in advance.

task.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Hi, there!

Today users are only able to self-register with OAUTH2 when general self-register is enabled.

The improvement would be:
- Allow users logging in with OAUTH2 accounts to self-register even when general self-registering is disabled;
- Allow admins to restrict users to OAUTH2 only, blocking users from creating and using passwords if they are coming from an OAUTH2 provider (this would allow a single action to suspend users in multiple Coolify instances);

This would allow us to, for example, control who can and cannot login into a Coolify instance using a tool like Authentik (ex: only users within a certain organization).

The ideal world would be SCIM 2.0 or something like LDAP, but this is a good start to block access whenever someone leaves the team.

Thanks in advance.

task.title: [Improvement]: Do not share all environment variables across all containers in a Compose project

data.title: [Improvement]: Do not share all environment variables across all containers in a Compose project
data.body:
### Problem Description

When deploying a Docker Compose project, Coolify injects all environment variables into every container, regardless of which container they were defined for.

From inspecting the "Raw Docker compose" in the UI, I believe this happens like this:
  1. A single `.env` file is created containing ALL environment variables from the entire Docker Compose project
  2. This `.env` file is automatically added to every container's `env_file:` directive

### Security Implications

This seems to be a serious security issue. 

Any container in a multi-container Docker Compose project can read the secrets/credentials of every other container.

Examples:
  - In a Next.js + PostgreSQL + Redis stack, the Redis container has access to POSTGRES_PASSWORD and the PostgreSQL container can see OPENAI_API_KEY meant only for the app
  - In any multi-database setup, each database container can see the credentials of other databases
  - If one container is compromised, an attacker gains credentials for all containers in the project

###  Expected Behavior

  Each container should only have access to:
  - Variables explicitly defined in its `environment: ` section
  - Variables from env files explicitly declared in its `env_file:` section
  - Coolify metadata variables (`COOLIFY_*`, `SERVICE_URL_*`, etc.) as needed

Variables specific to other containers (especially credentials) should NOT be accessible to unrelated containers.

### Coolify Version

v4.0.0-beta.452

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

Yes (Coolify Cloud)

### Coolify Version

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

Yes (Coolify Cloud)

task.title: [Enhancement]: Predefined server environment variables

data.title: [Enhancement]: Predefined server environment variables
data.body:
### Request Type

New Feature

### Description

When deploying a resource using the "Multiple Servers" option, currently there is no way to distinguish between the servers from within the application - because the container running on both servers get identical setup. 

This makes debugging harder as application logs cannot identify which server deployment an issue happened - take for instance a failure due to network errors happening specifically on one server (randomly). 

A way to define Environment variables for each server and making them available for each application deployed on that server, will fix this limitation (and also generalised in a way for use cases beyond what I described here).

A way to define Environment variables for each server and making them available for each application deployed on that server, will fix this limitation (and also generalised in a way for use cases beyond what I described here).

task.title: [Enhancement]: Integrated backups for Git based Docker Composes

data.title: [Enhancement]: Integrated backups for Git based Docker Composes
data.body:
### Request Type

New Feature

### Description


### Description

When deploying a Docker Compose file via GitHub App (using the `dockercompose` buildpack), database services are not detected and `ServiceDatabase` records are not created. This means automated backups are not available for databases in these deployments.

However, when using "Empty Docker Compose" or one-click services (like Supabase), database detection works correctly and backups are available.

### Current Behavior

| Deployment Method | Model | Creates ServiceDatabase | Backups Available |
|---|---|---|---|
| Empty Docker Compose | `Service` | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| GitHub App (dockercompose buildpack) | `Application` | ❌ No | ❌ No |
| One-click Services (e.g., Supabase) | `Service` | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |

### Expected Behavior

Database services in Docker Compose files deployed via GitHub App should be detected and have backup functionality available, similar to Empty Docker Compose deployments.

### Technical Details

The issue is in `bootstrap/helpers/shared.php` in the `parseDockerComposeFile()` function:

**Service model path (lines 1263-2025):**
- Calls `isDatabaseImage()` to detect databases
- Creates `ServiceDatabase` records for detected databases
- These databases can have scheduled backups

**Application model path (lines 2026-2767):**
- Does NOT call `isDatabaseImage()`
- Does NOT create `ServiceDatabase` records
- All services are treated as application containers
- No backup support

### Proposed Solution

Add database detection logic to the Application model parsing path:

1. In `parseDockerComposeFile()` for the Application model (around line 2066), add:
   ```php
   $isDatabase = isDatabaseImage($image, $service);
   data_set($service, 'is_database', $isDatabase);
   ```

2. Create `ServiceDatabase` records for detected database services, similar to how it's done in the Service model path.

3. Alternatively, consider refactoring to share the database detection logic between both paths.

### Use Case

Users deploying full-stack applications via GitHub (e.g., Next.js app + PostgreSQL + pgbouncer in a single compose file) expect database backups to work the same way as standalone database deployments or one-click services.

### Related Code

- `bootstrap/helpers/shared.php` - `parseDockerComposeFile()` function
- `bootstrap/helpers/docker.php` - `isDatabaseImage()` function
- `app/Models/ServiceDatabase.php` - Database model with backup support
- `app/Models/ScheduledDatabaseBackup.php` - Backup scheduling

### Environment

- Coolify version: latest
- Deployment method: GitHub App with dockercompose buildpack

### Request Type

New Feature

### Description


### Description

When deploying a Docker Compose file via GitHub App (using the `dockercompose` buildpack), database services are not detected and `ServiceDatabase` records are not created. This means automated backups are not available for databases in these deployments.

However, when using "Empty Docker Compose" or one-click services (like Supabase), database detection works correctly and backups are available.

### Current Behavior

| Deployment Method | Model | Creates ServiceDatabase | Backups Available |
|---|---|---|---|
| Empty Docker Compose | `Service` | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| GitHub App (dockercompose buildpack) | `Application` | ❌ No | ❌ No |
| One-click Services (e.g., Supabase) | `Service` | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |

### Expected Behavior

Database services in Docker Compose files deployed via GitHub App should be detected and have backup functionality available, similar to Empty Docker Compose deployments.

### Technical Details

The issue is in `bootstrap/helpers/shared.php` in the `parseDockerComposeFile()` function:

**Service model path (lines 1263-2025):**
- Calls `isDatabaseImage()` to detect databases
- Creates `ServiceDatabase` records for detected databases
- These databases can have scheduled backups

**Application model path (lines 2026-2767):**
- Does NOT call `isDatabaseImage()`
- Does NOT create `ServiceDatabase` records
- All services are treated as application containers
- No backup support

### Proposed Solution

Add database detection logic to the Application model parsing path:

1. In `parseDockerComposeFile()` for the Application model (around line 2066), add:
   ```php
   $isDatabase = isDatabaseImage($image, $service);
   data_set($service, 'is_database', $isDatabase);
   ```

2. Create `ServiceDatabase` records for detected database services, similar to how it's done in the Service model path.

3. Alternatively, consider refactoring to share the database detection logic between both paths.

### Use Case

Users deploying full-stack applications via GitHub (e.g., Next.js app + PostgreSQL + pgbouncer in a single compose file) expect database backups to work the same way as standalone database deployments or one-click services.

### Related Code

- `bootstrap/helpers/shared.php` - `parseDockerComposeFile()` function
- `bootstrap/helpers/docker.php` - `isDatabaseImage()` function
- `app/Models/ServiceDatabase.php` - Database model with backup support
- `app/Models/ScheduledDatabaseBackup.php` - Backup scheduling

### Environment

- Coolify version: latest
- Deployment method: GitHub App with dockercompose buildpack
